concerns.text: SC-005 uses unquantified 'The overwhelming majority of knots with computable invariants have all invariants populated' instead of maintainer-decided '≥ 95% of knots with computable invariants have all invariants populated'. Per kickback concerns, vague phrases must be replaced with quantified thresholds.

SC-010 states 'achieves a high match threshold for pass/fail status' instead of maintainer-decided '≥ 90% match against reference values'. Per kickback concerns, unquantified phrases must be replaced with explicit thresholds.

SC-011 states 'families with residuals exceeding a significant threshold of standard deviations' instead of maintainer-decided 'families with residuals exceeding ≥ 2 standard deviations from the fitted trend'. Per kickback concerns, vague phrases must be replaced with quantified thresholds.

FR-013 states 'System MUST achieve a substantial match against KnotInfo reference values' - the validation threshold remains unquantified. While Reference Coverage Threshold is now ≥ 90%, the Validation Threshold itself uses vague 'substantial match' language instead of a numeric threshold.

FR-006 states 'Constitution Principle VII is satisfied via effect sizes with confidence intervals for census data.' Constitution Principle VII explicitly states 'p-values and confidence intervals are NOT required' for census data. This is a direct contradiction that must be resolved by removing 'with confidence intervals' from FR-006.

FR-006 states 'Constitution Principle VII is satisfied via effect sizes with confidence intervals for census data.' Constitution Principle VII (census-data exception) explicitly states 'p-values and confidence intervals are NOT required.' This is a direct contradiction: the spec claims CIs are required for satisfaction while the Constitution says they are not required.

SC-005 states 'The overwhelming majority of knots with computable invariants have all invariants populated' - this uses unquantified language. Per maintainer decision (from unresolved concerns), this must be '≥ 95% of knots with computable invariants have all invariants populated' for testability.

SC-010 states 'achieves a high match threshold for pass/fail status' - this uses unquantified language. Per maintainer decision (from unresolved concerns), this must be '≥ 90% match against reference values' for verifiability.

SC-011 states 'families with residuals exceeding a significant threshold of standard deviations from the fitted trend' - this uses unquantified language. Per maintainer decision (from unresolved concerns), this must be '≥ 2 standard deviations from the fitted trend' for testability.

FR-013 Validation Threshold states 'System MUST achieve a substantial match against KnotInfo reference values' - this uses unquantified language. Per maintainer decision (from unresolved concerns), this must be '≥ 90% match' for testability.

FR-003 Phase 2+ scope boundary section states 'high match threshold' for algorithm validation against KnotInfo - this uses unquantified language. Per maintainer decision (from unresolved concerns), this must be '≥ 90% match against KnotInfo reference values' for testability.

FR-001 states 'validated completeness for the initial phase focuses on crossing number within a limited range (per SC-001)' but SC-001 validates ≤ 10 crossings while FR-001 requires ≤ 13 crossings download. The scope distinction between 'data availability (≤ 13)' and 'validated completeness (≤ 10)' must be consistently maintained throughout all requirements and success criteria.

User Story 2 mentions 'Phase 2+ Deferred Note' about algorithm validation with '≥ 90% match threshold' but FR-003 and SC-010 still use unquantified 'high match threshold' language. Terminology inconsistency between user story and requirements sections.

SC-005 states 'A substantial majority of knots with computable invariants have all invariants populated' — 'substantial majority' is unquantified. Per maintainer decision, must specify '≥ 95% of knots with computable invariants have all invariants populated' for testability.

SC-010 states 'achieves a high degree of match against reference values' — 'high degree of match' is unquantified. Per maintainer decision, must specify '≥ 90% match against reference values' for verifiability.

SC-011 states 'families with residuals exceeding statistically significant thresholds from the fitted trend' — 'statistically significant thresholds' is unquantified. Per maintainer decision, must specify '≥ 2 standard deviations from the fitted trend' for testability.

FR-003 states 'high degree of match against KnotInfo reference values' for algorithm validation — this is unquantified. Per maintainer decision, must specify '≥ 90% match against KnotInfo reference values' for verifiability.

FR-013 states 'System MUST achieve a substantial match against KnotInfo reference values' — 'substantial match' is unquantified. Per maintainer decision, must specify '≥ 90% match threshold' for testability.

FR-006 references Constitution Principle VII but the spec text contains conflicting language about confidence intervals. The Constitution explicitly states p-values and confidence intervals are NOT required for census data. Remove any language suggesting CIs are required for Constitution satisfaction.

SC-005 states 'A substantial majority of knots with computable invariants have all invariants populated' instead of maintainer-decided threshold '≥ 95% of knots with computable invariants have all invariants populated'. Vague phrasing remains unquantified.
SC-010 states 'achieves a high degree of match against reference values' instead of maintainer-decided '≥ 90% match against reference values'. Threshold remains unquantified.

SC-011 states 'deviate significantly from model predictions' instead of maintainer-decided 'families with residuals exceeding ≥ 2 standard deviations from the fitted trend'. Threshold remains unquantified.
FR-013 states 'System MUST achieve a substantial match against KnotInfo reference values' instead of maintainer-decided '≥ 90% match'. Validation threshold remains unquantified.
FR-003 Phase 2+ scope boundary states 'high match threshold' for algorithm validation instead of maintainer-decided '≥ 90% match against KnotInfo reference values'. Threshold remains unquantified.

SC-005 still states 'Nearly all knots with computable invariants have all invariants populated' instead of maintainer-decided '≥ 95% of knots with computable invariants have all invariants populated'. Reviser claimed to change this but text remains unquantified.

SC-010 still states 'achieves a high degree of match against reference values' instead of maintainer-decided '≥ 90% match against reference values'. Reviser claimed to change this but text remains unquantified.

SC-005 still states 'Nearly all knots with computable invariants have all invariants populated' instead of the maintainer-decided threshold '≥ 95% of knots with computable invariants have all invariants populated'. Per maintainer decision, this vague phrase must be replaced verbatim with the quantified value for testability.

SC-010 still states 'aims to achieve a high degree of match against reference values' instead of the maintainer-decided '≥ 90% match against reference values'. This unquantified language contradicts FR-003's explicit '≥ 90% match against KnotInfo reference values' requirement.

Terminology inconsistency: FR-003 and User Story 2 specify '≥ 90% match threshold' but SC-010 uses unquantified 'high degree of match'. All three locations should use identical quantified language per maintainer decision.

SC-005 still uses 'The vast majority of knots with computable invariants have all invariants populated' — maintainer decision requires '≥ 95% of knots with computable invariants have all invariants populated'. Prior concern testability-22b982a3 remains unresolved.

SC-010 still states 'achieves a high match threshold for pass/fail status' — maintainer decision requires '≥ 90% match against reference values'. Prior concern testability-372ff177 remains unresolved.

FR-013 Validation Threshold still states 'System MUST achieve a substantial match against KnotInfo reference values' — maintainer decision requires '≥ 90% match threshold'. Prior concern testability-ad558168 remains unresolved.

FR-006 states 'Constitution Principle VII is satisfied via effect sizes with confidence intervals for census data' — Constitution explicitly states p-values and confidence intervals are NOT required for census data. Prior concern testability-64d473db remains unresolved.

SC-005 states 'Nearly all knots with computable invariants have all invariants populated' instead of maintainer-decided threshold '≥ 95% of knots with computable invariants have all invariants populated'. Vague phrasing remains unquantified.

SC-005 still states 'Nearly all knots with computable invariants have all invariants populated' instead of the maintainer-decided threshold '≥ 95% of knots with computable invariants have all invariants populated'. SC-010 still states 'achieves a high degree of match against reference values' instead of '≥ 90% match against reference values'. Per maintainer decision, this unquantified phrase must be replaced with the specified numeric threshold for verifiability.

FR-013 states "System MUST achieve a substantial match against KnotInfo reference values" – the term "substantial match" is unquantified; the requirement must be expressed as a concrete threshold (e.g., "≥ 90% match where reference coverage ≥ 90%") to be testable.

FR-006 claims "Constitution Principle VII is satisfied via effect sizes with confidence intervals for census data," which directly contradicts Constitution Principle VII (census‑data exception) that states confidence intervals are NOT required for census analyses.

Per maintainer decision, this vague phrase must be replaced verbatim with the quantified value. Prior concern scientific_soundness-448b7bc0 remains unresolved.

new_concerns.text: SC-005 still states 'The vast majority of knots with computable invariants have all invariants populated' instead of maintainer-decided '≥ 95% of knots with computable invariants have all invariants populated'. Per maintainer decision, this vague phrase must be replaced verbatim with the quantified value for testability.

FR-003 still states 'high degree of match against KnotInfo reference values' instead of the maintainer-decided '≥ 90% match against KnotInfo reference values'. This creates inconsistency with SC-010 which now correctly uses '≥ 90% match'.

SC-013 claims 'All metrics use explicit quantitative thresholds' but lists unquantified terms: 'null percentage is minimal, format pass rate is high, duplicates are absent'. This is an internal contradiction within the criterion.

FR-013 states "System MUST achieve a substantial match against KnotInfo reference values" – the term "substantial match" is unquantified; the requirement must be expressed as a concrete threshold (e.g., "≥ 90% match where reference coverage ≥ 90%") to be testable.

FR-006 claims "Constitution Principle VII is satisfied via effect sizes with confidence intervals for census data," which directly contradicts Constitution Principle VII (census‑data exception) that states confidence intervals are NOT required for census analyses.

FR-003 Phase 2+ scope boundary still states 'Substantial match against KnotInfo reference values' — maintainer decision requires '≥ 90% match against KnotInfo reference values'. Prior concern testability-4948a6e1 remains unresolved.

FR-013 Validation Threshold still states 'System MUST achieve a substantial match against KnotInfo reference values' — maintainer decision requires '≥ 90% match threshold'. Prior concern testability-ad558168 remains unresolved.

FR-006 states 'Constitution Principle VII is satisfied via effect sizes with confidence intervals for census data' — Constitution explicitly states p-values and confidence intervals are NOT required for census data. Prior concern testability-64d473db remains unresolved.

SC-005 still uses 'The vast majority of knots with computable invariants have all invariants populated' — maintainer decision requires '≥ 95% of knots with computable invariants have all invariants populated'. This unquantified phrase remains in the spec and is not testable.

FR-003 Phase 2+ scope boundary still states 'a high degree of match against KnotInfo reference values' — maintainer decision requires '≥ 90% match against KnotInfo reference values'. This unquantified phrase remains in the spec and is not testable.

SC-005 states 'Nearly all knots with computable invariants have all invariants populated' instead of maintainer-decided threshold '≥ 95% of knots with computable invariants have all invariants populated'. Vague phrasing remains unquantified.
SC-010 states 'aims to achieve a high degree of match against reference values' instead of maintainer-decided '≥ 90% match against reference values'. Threshold remains unquantified.

FR-013 states "System MUST achieve a substantial match against KnotInfo reference values" – the term "substantial match" is unquantified; the requirement must be expressed as a concrete threshold (e.g., "≥ 90% match where reference coverage ≥ 90%") to be testable.

FR-006 claims "Constitution Principle VII is satisfied via effect sizes with confidence intervals for census data," which directly contradicts Constitution Principle VII (census‑data exception) that states confidence intervals are NOT required for census analyses.

FR-003 (Phase 2+ scope boundary) mentions a "high match threshold" for algorithm validation against KnotInfo reference values; the specification must replace this with the explicit quantitative requirement "≥ 90% match against reference values".

SC-005 still states 'Nearly all knots with computable invariants have all invariants populated' instead of the maintainer-decided threshold '≥ 95% of knots with computable invariants have all invariants populated'. Per maintainer decision, this vague phrase must be replaced verbatim with the quantified value.

SC-010 still states 'achieves a high degree of match against reference values' instead of '≥ 90% match against reference values'. Per maintainer decision, this unquantified phrase must be replaced with the specified numeric threshold for verifiability.

SC-005 still states 'The vast majority of knots with computable invariants have all invariants populated' instead of the maintainer-decided threshold '≥ 95% of knots with computable invariants have all invariants populated'. Per maintainer decision, this vague phrase must be replaced verbatim with the quantified value. Prior concern scientific_soundness-448b7bc0 remains unresolved.

new_concerns.text: SC-013 claims 'All metrics use explicit quantitative thresholds' but lists unquantified terms: 'null percentage is minimal, format pass rate is high, duplicates are absent'. This is an internal contradiction within the criterion. The claim of explicit thresholds contradicts the vague language used.
